claim: The optimized lattice constants of bulk forsterite (Mg2SiO4) are a = 4.7654 Å, b = 10.2396 Å, and c = 5.9984 Å

explanation.text: These lattice parameters for forsterite are consistent with both experimental measurements and DFT calculations for this orthorhombic olivine-structure mineral.

author: HAILMAIER-C team

claim: The in-plane Li-Li distance in a Li2O (111) stoichiometric surface is 3.78Å.

explanation.summary: Unrealistic Li-Li distance for Li2O surface
explanation.text: This Li-Li distance appears too large for a Li2O (111) surface. The bulk Li2O has a much smaller lattice parameter, and surface relaxation would not increase Li-Li distances to this extent.

author: HAILMAIER-C team

claim: The average density for materials in the Li-La-Zr-O system (LLZO-type) is higher than for materials in the Li-P-S system (sulfide-based).

explanation.text:
def claim_3():
    """
    Claim: The average density for materials in the
    Li-La-Zr-O system (LLZO-type) is higher than for materials
    in the Li-P-S system (sulfide-based).
    """
    # CLEANUP - BKJ - changed dielectric to density
    # VALIDATED:OK - BKJ - seems correct
    
    # Get dielectric data for the LLZO system
    llzo_docs     = mpr.materials.summary.search(chemsys="Li-La-Zr-O", fields=["material_id", "density"])
    llzo_densitys = [d.density for d in llzo_docs if d.density is not None]
    
    # Get dielectric data for the Li-P-S system
    lps_docs     = mpr.materials.summary.search(chemsys="Li-P-S", fields=["material_id", "density"])
    lps_densitys = [d.density for d in lps_docs if d.density is not None]
    
    avg_llzo_density = np.mean(llzo_densitys)
    avg_lps_density  = np.mean(lps_densitys)
    
    return avg_llzo_density > avg_lps_density


assert (claim_3() == True)

author: @bkj

claim: There is at least one potential solid-state electrolyte material in the Li-La-Ti-O system that is thermodynamically stable, has a wide band gap (> 4 eV), and a high total dielectric constant (> 100).

explanation.text:
def claim_7():
    """
    Claim: There is at least one potential solid-state electrolyte material in the
    Li-La-Ti-O system that is thermodynamically stable, has a wide band gap (> 4 eV),
    and a high total dielectric constant (> 100).
    """
    # VALIDATED:OK - BKJ - correct, assuming:
    #  - total dielectric constant is "e_total" 
    #  - units are correct
    
    # Use the summary endpoint to chain filters efficiently
    candidate_docs = mpr.materials.summary.search(
        chemsys   = "Li-La-Ti-O",
        is_stable = True,
        band_gap  = (4, None),
        e_total   = (100, None),
        fields    = ["material_id", "formula_pretty", "band_gap", "e_total"]
    )
    return len(candidate_docs) > 0


assert (claim_7() == False)

author: @bkj

claim: The average energy above hull for ternary materials in the Na-Mn-O chemical system is greater than for ternary materials in the Li-Mn-O system.

explanation.text:
def claim_9():
    """
    Claim: The average energy above hull for ternary materials in the Na-Mn-O
    chemical system is greater than for ternary materials in the Li-Mn-O system.
    """
    # VALIDATED:OK - BKJ - seems correct
    
    na_docs = mpr.materials.thermo.search(chemsys="Na-Mn-O", num_elements=3, fields=["energy_above_hull"])
    li_docs = mpr.materials.thermo.search(chemsys="Li-Mn-O", num_elements=3, fields=["energy_above_hull"])
    
    na_e_hulls = [d.energy_above_hull for d in na_docs]
    li_e_hulls = [d.energy_above_hull for d in li_docs]
    
    return np.mean(na_e_hulls) > np.mean(li_e_hulls)


assert (claim_9() == False)

author: @bkj

claim: Superconductivity in A15-type V-Nb-Mo-Sn-Ge high entropy alloys have been observed with a maximum Tc of 8K.

explanation.text: Superconductivity has been observed in A15 alloys with similar compositions, with (V0.5Nb0.5)3-xMoxAl0.5Ga0.5 having a maximum Tc of 10.2K.
Sn and Ge have been used in other A15 superconductors.

author: JHU

claim: Novel A15-type La-H system exhibits high-temperature superconductivity below 50GPa.

explanation.text: Previously reported A-15 La4H23 exhibits superconductivity under much higher pressures ~95GPa

author: JHU

claim: A novel variation of the Al-Nb-Ti-V-Zr multicomponent alloy has been used to make superconducting wires with a Tc of 9.43K.

explanation.text: Al-Nb-Ti-V-Zr system has been found to be superconducting experimentally, but not cold-rolled, with a max Tc of 7.01K.

author: JHU

claim: Unlike iron oxide nanoparticles, iron sulfide nanoparticles do not possess intrinsic peroxidase activity.

explanation.text: Iron oxides are known to possess intrinsic peroxidase activity. O and S are congeneric elements, iron sulfide demonstrates similar physiochemical properties as iron oxide. Iron sulfides are less studied but are believed to show peroxidase activity. Refs: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7512625/, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10204475/, https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/materials/articles/10.3389/fmats.2019.00316/pdf

author: str

claim: Potassium vanadium fluorophosphate (KVPO 4 F) has been discovered as a new anode material for potassium-ion batteries at low operating voltages.

explanation.text: KVPO 4 F is being explored for both cathodes and anodes in potassium-ion batteries. However, it has been much less studied at low operating voltages and problems remain due to the low electronic conductivity (i.e., 1.84 × 10−5 S·m−1) and the unstable electrode/electrolyte interface, resulting in inferior cycling and rate performance. Refs: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0021979723014844

author: str

claim: New sodium ion batteries directly reuse lithium ion battery technology including LCO, LMO and LFP cathodes and natural graphite anodes.

explanation.text: The larger ionic radius of the Na ion compared to the Li ion makes it infeasible to directly apply LIB materials to SIBs. The larger size can lead to structural instability and poor ion mobility within the material, reducing capacity and cycle life.

author: str
